_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Excision of Multiple Sebaceous Cysts over scrotum
specialty: General Surgery, Pediatric Surgery, Genitourinary Surgery
clinical_indications: Obstruction to one of the sebaceous ducts, resulting in accumulation of sebaceous material
Gold standard treatment for epidermal cyst is complete excision of cyst and its contents
Incision and avulsion of cyst with the wall is indicated
Excision of small cysts along with the skin

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure and planned line of management
Scrotal ultrasound (not required for sebaceous cysts)

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?
Did clinical evaluation and Scrotal ultrasound/FNAC confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing or incomplete, lacking detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, or indication for procedure
Diagnosis not confirmed by clinical evaluation (Scrotal ultrasound is not required for sebaceous cysts)
Detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details missing or incomplete
Detailed procedure / Operative Notes not available
Discharge summary with follow-up advice not provided

clinical_key_pointers: Sebaceous cysts (epidermal cysts) are common in the scrotal skin. This occurs due to obstruction to one of the sebaceous ducts, resulting in accumulation of sebaceous material. They are usually small and multiple (Strawberry scrotum). On clinical examination, they are localized to scrotal skin and separate from the testes.
Clinical features: They are slow-growing and appear in early adulthood or middle age

Clinical features: Hemispherical or spherical swelling located in the dermis. A dark spot in the centre (punctum) filled with keratin is a diagnostic feature of this cyst. The punctum indicates blockage of the duct.
Clinical features: In 20-30% of cases, instead of opening into the skin, sebaceous duct opens into the hair follicle. Hence, punctum is not seen
Clinical features: It has a smooth surface, round borders, soft and putty consistency and is nontender
Clinical features: The swelling is mobile over the deep structures, and the skin is free all around except an area of adherence at the site of punctum
surgical_options: Gold standard treatment for epidermal cyst is complete excision of cyst and its contents
Incision and avulsion of cyst with the wall. Care should be taken to excise the entire cyst wall to prevent recurrence.
When it is small it can be excised along with the skin.
